purpose: Show the user where to buy the same item for the best price, with enough merchant context to decide at a glance.
primaryUserGoal: Pick the best merchant for a specific item based on price, stock, and shipping.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to compare prices, find the cheapest store, or is evaluating where to buy a specific named product.
goodFor: Comparing a named product across 3-8 merchants
Confirming best current price on a watched item
supports: Price / stock / shipping columns with tone
idealDataShape: Merchant name, price, stock state, shipping summary

populationInstructions.summary: Populate one row per merchant selling the target product. Use the leadingImage field for merchant logos (query by merchant name). Mark the cheapest current option with tone='success' and emphasis=true.
steps: Identify the specific product (name, model number, size if relevant).
List merchants that carry it (3-8 is ideal).
For each merchant, capture: price, in-stock state, shipping cost/speed.
Mark the best current price with tone='success' and emphasis=true.
Include a merchant logo image descriptor (query: '<merchant> logo') for leadingImage.
guardrails: Never make up prices — only include verified data.
Always note the timestamp or freshness of the price data in a chip or note.
Don't include a merchant if it's out of stock unless asked to show all options.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_table_rows to refresh individual merchant rows
Use set_scope_tags to toggle filters like 'in-stock only'
replaceTriggers: User asks to compare a different product
